data.name: Mom's birthday
data.brief: Mom turns 68. Need to plan a visit or call + gift.

data.name: Default to yes for family time
data.content: When uncertain whether to commit family time, default to yes. The marginal cost of skipping work is low; the marginal cost of skipping a 5-year-old's everything is unrecoverable.

data.name: Capture input separately from output
data.content: Don't let inbox-style capture pollute the work surface. Capture cheap and fast; process and synthesize on a separate cadence.

data.name: No meetings on Fridays
data.content: Friday is a maker day. Decline anything that isn't on fire. Hold the line — it pays back the rest of the week.

data.name: Theo loves Islay single malts (esp. Lagavulin)
data.content: For Theo: peated Islay over speyside every time. Lagavulin 16 is the safe gift; Ardbeg Uigeadail or Laphroaig 10 Cask Strength upgrades. Loathes blended.

data.name: Priya is vegetarian + dairy-free
data.content: Vegetarian and lactose-intolerant. Loves Levantine food. When hosting: hummus + warm pita, mushroom dishes, anything roasted. Avoid cheese and creamy sauces.

data.name: Mom prefers phone calls over texts
data.content: Mom answers texts a day late but loves a 15-minute Sunday afternoon call. Don't ask her to FaceTime — she gets flustered with the camera.

data.name: Marco's coffee taste — Sey, Onyx, light roasts
data.content: Marco only drinks single-origin light roasts. Sey, Onyx Coffee Lab, Tim Wendelboe are home runs. Avoid anything dark or blended. Beans, not pre-ground.

data.name: Hannah collects 35mm film cameras
data.content: Hannah is into film photography — has a Pentax K1000 and looking for a Contax T2 or Olympus XA. Birthday is May 30.

data.name: Journal — May 12
data.content: Long Monday. Lumen redesign scope creep is making me wonder if I'm choosing the right battles. Maya gave me the talk again about not absorbing other people's anxiety. Quiet Coach onboarding is sitting in my head — I should sketch it tomorrow before email.

data.name: Journal — May 13
data.content: Therapy. Sam preschool conversation is bigger than it looks. Lia said something that stuck: 'You're not picking a preschool, you're picking a community.' I think that reframes which questions actually matter.

data.name: Mid-30s energy is non-linear
data.content: Energy now is not flat over the day or week — it's spiky. Two hours of deep focus before email is worth a full afternoon of meeting fog. Designing my week around two protected mornings (Tue, Thu) keeps everything else solvable.

data.name: Preschool = picking a community, not a curriculum
data.content: The Montessori vs. play-based debate matters less than which families we'll be spending Saturdays with for the next five years. Optimize for the parents we'll like, the friends Sam will grow up with — curriculum diffs disappear in elementary.

data.name: Maya recharges in motion, not stillness
data.content: I get my energy from quiet mornings; Maya gets hers from movement and being around people. After her promotion week, she didn't want a quiet weekend — she wanted a busy one. Plan her recharge accordingly.

data.name: Q1 2026 reflection
data.content: What worked: morning pages stuck (60-day streak); QC found its onboarding shape. What didn't: deep work block fell off in March during Lumen kickoff. Pattern: I let work soak up the morning protected time. Fix: defend Tue/Thu mornings absolutely.

data.name: Should Quiet Coach pivot to B2B?
data.content: Three early users asked if it could be deployed inside their company. Compelling but a totally different product. Worth a real exploration sprint, or distraction from the consumer thesis?

data.name: Which preschool for Sam?
data.content: Sunny Lane (Noa's, easy continuity) vs. Greenfield Co-op (closer community, parent involvement) vs. Lighthouse Montessori (formal curriculum). Decision needed by June 15.

data.name: Decided: NYC Half this October
data.content: Registered for the Oct 4 NYC Half. 18-week training plan starts Apr 1. Sub-2 hour goal.

data.name: QC onboarding: start with a real problem
data.content: Don't ask users for goals abstractly — ask them what's currently bugging them this week. Coach the smallest concrete thing first. Concrete -> trust -> deeper.

data.name: Rilke — live the questions
data.content: Live the questions now. Perhaps you will then gradually, without noticing it, live along some distant day into the answer. — Rilke, Letters to a Young Poet

data.name: Dream: a cabin in the Hudson Valley
data.content: Year-round small cabin, walking distance to a town, room for Maya's parents to visit. Five-year horizon, not now.

data.name: Reflection: 8 years with Maya
data.content: What worked: protecting Sunday dinner, weekly date night, named recharge time. What needs work: I default to introvert when she's stressed; she needs the opposite. Be deliberate about that.

data.brief: Promised at his birthday dinner. He's into mental-models books.

data.name: Lumen Q2 kickoff notes
data.content: Kickoff for Q2 redesign. Attendees: Alex, Jordan, Bea, plus client side. Risk: scope creep — already adding 'small' merchandising widget to phase 1. Bea will draft the cut-line memo. ...

data.name: Voice memo — onboarding flash
data.content: Walking to coffee. What if the onboarding doesn't ask 'what's your goal' — it asks 'what's bugging you this week.' Concrete pain first. Trust builds. Goal abstraction comes later. ...

data.content: Priya's baby shower at Lia's. Saturday April 12 2pm.

data.name: Web clip — James Clear identity habits
data.content: Every action you take is a vote for the type of person you wish to become. The goal is not to read a book — the goal is to become a reader. ...

data.content: Summary: Mom proposes 2nd weekend in June, Fri-Sun via Amtrak. Bringing Noa a puzzle. Action: confirm date and pickup logistics.
